name: Proto-Uralic (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Uralic is the reconstructed common ancestor of the Uralic family (Finnic, Samic, Ugric and the other branches). It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Sammallahti, P.
sources.title: Historical phonology of the Uralic languages
sources.notes: Reference for Uralic branching (structural ancestry node only).

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the Uralic family — a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.
